title: 3D 1v1 FPS Battle Game with Weapons and AI Difficulty

requirements.text: Create a new React Three Fiber / Three.js game hook `use1v1Lol3DGame.ts` that implements a 3D first-person/third-person 1v1 battle game. The hook must manage: player state (position, HP, current weapon, ammo), AI bot state (position, HP, current weapon, ammo, difficulty), projectile physics, and collision detection in a 3D arena.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook exports game state (player, bot, projectiles, gamePhase, score) and action handlers
Player and bot each have HP that decreases when hit by projectiles
Game phase transitions: idle → playing → game-over
All state updates occur inside a requestAnimationFrame game loop

requirements.text: Implement four weapons in the game hook, switchable via number keys 1–4: Sniper (1 bullet, slow reload ~2.5s, high damage ~80), Shotgun (5 shots, medium reload ~1.5s, spread pattern, medium damage ~15 per pellet × 6 pellets), Pistol (20 shots, fast reload ~0.8s, medium damage ~25), Machine Gun (25 bullets, medium reload ~1.2s, rapid fire, lower damage ~12 per bullet). Each weapon tracks current ammo and reload state.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing keys 1, 2, 3, 4 switches to Sniper, Shotgun, Pistol, Machine Gun respectively
Each weapon has correct ammo capacity and reload timer
Firing is blocked when ammo is 0 and weapon is reloading
Shotgun fires multiple pellets in a spread pattern
HUD displays current weapon name, ammo count, and reload progress

requirements.text: Implement four AI difficulty levels (Easy, Medium, Hard, Extreme) that scale by reaction time and accuracy. Easy: 1500ms reaction delay, 40% accuracy. Medium: 800ms reaction, 60% accuracy. Hard: 350ms reaction, 80% accuracy. Extreme: 80ms reaction, 95% accuracy. The AI bot dynamically switches weapons based on distance to player (Sniper at long range, Shotgun at close range, Pistol/Machine Gun at medium range) with weapon switching thresholds scaled by difficulty. Player selects difficulty on a pre-game screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Difficulty selection screen appears before the game starts with Easy/Medium/Hard/Extreme options
AI reaction time matches the specified delay per difficulty
AI aim accuracy scales with difficulty (higher difficulty = tighter shot spread)
AI switches to Sniper when player is far, Shotgun when close, Pistol/MachineGun at medium range
AI reloads weapons when empty just like the player

requirements.text: Build a 3D arena scene in the page component `OneLolGame.tsx` using React Three Fiber. The arena should include a flat ground plane, perimeter walls, and several cover obstacles (boxes/pillars) scattered around. Use pointer-lock controls or orbit-style WASD movement so the player can navigate the arena in 3D. Render the AI bot as a visible 3D mesh that moves around the arena. Display projectile meshes (small spheres or cylinders) that travel through the scene.
acceptanceCriteria: Arena renders in 3D with ground, walls, and at least 6 cover obstacles
Player can move with WASD and look with mouse (pointer lock)
AI bot is represented by a 3D mesh (e.g., box or capsule) that moves toward/away from player
Projectiles are visible as small 3D meshes traveling from shooter to target
Game renders at 60fps with no major visual glitches

requirements.text: Add an in-game HUD overlaid on the 3D canvas showing: player HP bar, AI bot HP bar, current weapon name and ammo (e.g. 'Pistol 18/20'), reload progress bar when reloading, selected difficulty label, and a crosshair in the center of the screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Player HP bar and AI HP bar are both visible during gameplay
Weapon name and ammo count update immediately on switching or firing
Reload progress bar appears and fills during reload, disappears when complete
Crosshair is centered on screen at all times during gameplay
Difficulty label is visible during the match

requirements.text: Show a game-over screen when either the player or AI bot reaches 0 HP. Display who won, the match result, and a 'Play Again' button that resets the game (returning to difficulty selection). Integrate score submission using the existing ScoreSubmission component when the player wins.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-over screen appears immediately when HP reaches 0
Screen clearly states 'You Win' or 'You Lose'
'Play Again' button returns to the difficulty selection screen and resets all state
Score submission form appears on win screen
Leaderboard is shown below score submission on win

constraints: All game logic must remain in frontend hooks and page components — no backend changes required
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Reuse existing ScoreSubmission and Leaderboard components from frontend/src/components/
The route and nav link for the 1v1 lol game already exists in App.tsx and Layout.tsx — do not duplicate it
nonGoals: Multiplayer / networked play — AI opponent only
Mobile touch controls for the 3D game
